title: Podman Upgrade caused issues with containers
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

RHEL 7.9 host has podman version 0.4.1.
I tried to upgrade using following cmds:
```
sudo subscription-manager repos --enable=rhel-7-server-extras-rpms
sudo yum -y update podman
```
podman got updated to version 1.6.4

Following is the output of the update.
```

[root@RHEL75SRV ~]#
[root@RHEL75SRV ~]# podman --version
podman version 1.6.4
```

The Apps on my Containers were accessible however when I tried to get inside the container, I get the error.

```
[root@RHEL75SRV ~]# podman exec -it Manzoom-dev bash
Error: exec failed: container "3c83520e6934b8695fd2d49d3dd7757ed4e62f50ba0c5947ca492ba663afceb3" does not exist: OCI runtime error
```

I restarted the podman host. Then I noticed that my containers went in "created" STATUS!

```
[root@RHEL75SRV tmp]# podman ps -a | head -3
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                             COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS   PORTS                                                                         NAMES
65e9a23158b9  docker.io/couchbase/server:6.6.5  /root/tmp/config-...  2 minutes ago  Created  10.10.32.33:8091-8096->8091-8096/tcp                                          pratikmehtatest2
a9022660b6d6  docker.io/couchbase/server:6.6.4                        6 days ago     Created  10.10.20.29:8091-8096->8091-8096/tcp, 10.10.20.29:8091-8096->8091-8096/udp    parasuram-dev
```

Error messages:

```
[root@RHEL75SRV tmp]# podman start Pratima-dev
Error: unable to start container "Pratima-dev": container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:297: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"Invalid slice name libpod_parent\"": OCI runtime error

[root@RHEL75SRV tmp]# podman stop Pratima-dev
Error: can only stop created or running containers. 699e6eff80bfde5589c08d87535d68c83c40e5132a97f660b2d147444d0c4980 is in state configured: container state improper

# docker run -d --cpus="2" --memory="4g"  -v /root:/root/tmp/ -v /data1/docker_containers/pratikmehtatest2:/opt/couchbase/var --name pratikmehtatest2 -p 10.10.32.33:8091-8096:8091-8096 -p 10.10.32.33:9100-9105:9100-9105 -p 10.10.32.33:11210-11211:11210-11211 couchbase/server:6.6.5 /root/tmp/config-cb.sh
Error: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:59: mounting \\\"/var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/65e9a23158b9154a55c57065b31e417f2eb12368084d2375383d0c5dc7089829/userdata/.containerenv\\\" to rootfs \\\"/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/157de62e243fee51f564a86e5ce8372fa1b66ece6766baf24aaa2fb8f3884bb7/merged\\\" at \\\"/run/.containerenv\\\" caused \\\"mount through procfd: possibly malicious path detected -- refusing to operate on /run/.containerenv\\\"\"": OCI runtime error
https://access.redhat.com/support/cases/#/case/03266569/discussion?commentId=a0a6R00000TY5PKQA1
```

I tried uninstalling the podman 1.6.4 and installing 0.4.1, however same error!
Please help on how can I resolve this ?



**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

The only latest version I found on RHEL 7.9 is Podman version 1.6.4.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical server

comments.body:
Upstream development can only support the latest podman versions, v4.1.1 at the moment.

If you have problems with RHEL please reach out to your Red Hat support channels.

Hi @Luap99 , I am trying to update my podman to latest version by doing "yum update podman", however it only goes to 1.6.4.
This does not seem to be an RHEL problem.
The podman fails to work after the update. Who can assist ?

RHEL 7 only has v1.6.4, if you want to use newer versions you have to update to RHEL 8 or 9.

If you have an issue with Podman on RHEL connect the Red Hat support or open a bugzilla.
Please open a bugzilla or call support. that is the proper approach here.
